name: Cobeña, Daganzo de Arriba, Paracuellos del Jarama – circular route
name_original: Cobeña, Daganzo de Arriba, Paracuellos del Jarama – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cobeña are situated within the Campiña region of Madrid, characterized by open countryside and moderate elevation changes. The area features well-paved surfaces suitable for road cycling, offering diverse terrains for various fitness levels. Natural features like the Jarama River and the Viñuelas Stream provide scenic points along many routes. This landscape allows for both leisurely rides and more demanding training circuits. Best road cycling routes around Cobeña The most popular road cycling route is Cobeña, Daganzo de Arriba, Paracuellos del Jarama – circular route, a 20.5 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects Cobeña with Daganzo de Arriba and Paracuellos del Jarama, featuring around 1,300 feet (398 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Vellón – Viewpoint of El Casar loop from Av. Paz - Urb. Miraval, a moderate 50.8 miles (81.7 km) path. This longer route explores the Campiña area, offering varied terrain and significant elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Algete Bullring – Algete Town Center loop from Algete, a 51.7 miles (83.1 km) trail leading through the open countryside of the Campiña region, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Cobeña is defined by open countryside, moderate elevation changes, and well-paved roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to more challenging circuits. The routes in Cobeña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 2,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cobeña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cobeña?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Cobeña, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Cobeña?
A: The road cycling routes in Cobeña cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 9 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 8 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for everyone from beginners to experienced riders.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while road cycling in the Cobeña area?
A: Road cycling in Cobeña offers scenic views of the Campiña region, characterized by open countryside and moderate elevation changes. Routes often feature natural elements like the Jarama River and the Viñuelas Stream, providing pleasant backdrops for your ride.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the road cycling routes in Cobeña?
A: Yes, several routes pass by notable features. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/293382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viñuelas Stream Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1614034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto de Viñuelas Wall Passage&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/687106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ford of the Jarama River&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque spots to pause and enjoy the surroundings.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Cobeña?
A: Yes, circular routes are popular in the Cobeña area. A well-known option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979868600/cobena-daganzo-de-arriba-paracuellos-del-jarama-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cobeña, Daganzo de Arriba, Paracuellos del Jarama – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 32.9 km long and features around 398 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some longer or more challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38252857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vellón – Viewpoint of El Casar loop from Av. Paz - Urb. Miraval&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans 81.7 km and includes significant elevation changes, exploring more of the Campiña area.

Q: Are there options for beginner road cyclists in Cobeña?
A: Absolutely. Cobeña offers several easy and moderate routes with well-paved surfaces, ideal for beginners. These routes allow you to enjoy the open countryside without overly strenuous climbs. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' in the guide.

Q: Is there parking available for road cyclists in Cobeña?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists aren't detailed in the route information, Cobeña, as a town, generally offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival or consider starting your ride from a location with known parking facilities.

Q: Can I reach the road cycling routes in Cobeña using public transport?
A: Cobeña is part of the Madrid region's transport network. While direct public transport access to specific trailheads might vary, buses connect Cobeña to larger towns in the area, which may offer more extensive public transport links. Planning your route start and end points with public transport in mind is recommended.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Cobeña?
A: The Campiña region around Cobeña is generally pleasant for road cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially midday, while winter offers cooler conditions, but routes remain accessible due to the well-paved surfaces.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cobeña?
A: The road cycling routes around Cobeña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,700 road cyclists have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain and the blend of accessible paths with more challenging circuits.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes in the Cobeña area connect to or pass through towns like Daganzo de Arriba, Paracuellos del Jarama, or Algete. These towns typically offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments and breaks during your ride.

Q: Can I find quiet road cycling routes away from traffic in Cobeña?
A: The Campiña region is known for its open countryside, and many road cycling routes utilize quieter rural roads. While some sections may pass through villages, the overall experience often provides a peaceful ride away from heavy traffic, especially on routes that venture further into the agricultural landscapes.
tips: The natural environment is one of the attractions of the town due to the cereal steppe, the riverside forest and the gentle hills and slopes. You can see a lot of birds in the area (great bustard, montagu's harrier, lesser kestrel, black vulture).
Excellent views of the city and the airport. Especially for airplane lovers, it is a very friendly place to enjoy landing and taking off.
